assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: The model explicitly sections the checklists to leverage the Dev (Rapid) clusters for testing prior to the Prod (Stable) upgrades, and recommends prod-specific maintenance windows.

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: The model mentions the lack of node management (no cordoning/draining) but fails to mention the lack of SSH access or the requirement for mandatory resource requests.

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: The model mentions checking for API deprecations using the GKE Console, kubent, and Pluto, but does not provide a kubectl command to check for deprecations.

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: The model does not mention mandatory resource requests anywhere in the response.

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: The model includes Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) but completely omits checking for bare pods and graceful shutdown configurations.

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: The model includes the command `kubectl get nodes` to verify that nodes are on version 1.32.x.

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: The model provides a 'Workload Health Checks' section, including a command to check pod status and instructions to check Ingress, Networking, and HPAs.

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: The model explicitly advises running regression and CI/CD tests against the Dev clusters first to ensure the core workloads will survive the Prod upgrade.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: The model does not mention the GKE release schedule, the 1-month advance notice, or the 72-hour notification.
The model includes an 'Observability & Logs' section advising the user to check Cloud Logging for error spikes and verify agent logs.
